features: Covers operations, logistics, supply chain processes, and resource coordination through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in logistics, operations, supply chain management, and process improvement.

name: Seafood Handling
description: Students will be equipped with essential knowledge and skills to manage the supply of seafood and maintain facilities associated with the processing and retail of fish and shellfish. In this module, students will be exposed to the principles of basic hygiene, as well as seafood and general food safety in accordance to industry regulations. In addition, students will examine the environmental issues related to seafood processing and learn about the planning and operational matters on fisheries management.
features: Covers food, nutrition, wellness, and healthy lifestyle choices through real-world applications.
suitableFor: Students interested in food, nutrition, wellness, and making healthier lifestyle decisions.

name: Environmental Data Analysis
description: The module introduces students to environmental data analysis based on statistical concepts including hypothesis testing, correlation analysis, regression, and analysis of variance. Students will learn the importance of good data quality and apply appropriate data cleansing and transformation techniques to organise and prepare data for analysis. Through the application of large-scale environmental data, students will learn to process information with data visualisation software such as Tableau, and effectively present the results in interactive graphics. This module also covers Geographic Information Systems (GIS), which is a computer-based tool that stores, manipulates and visualises geographic information and its application in the environmental industry.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility through laboratory practice and real-world applications.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Aquatic Animal Health and Nutrition
description: The module aims to equip students with an understanding of fish health issues and feed nutrition requirements that are relevant to aquaculture. Students will experimentally formulate feeds and critically assess their performance, while also integrating bio-security fundamentals in their experimental design. They will be introduced to common aquatic diseases in aquaculture, including disease diagnosis, control, and treatment. Students will also study concepts of nutritional and health science and understand how to apply these concepts in different life cycle stages of fishes and crustaceans.
features: Covers food, nutrition, wellness, and healthy lifestyle choices through hands-on activities and laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in food, nutrition, wellness, and making healthier lifestyle decisions.

name: Genomics
description:
Genomics is a module that allows students to explore the vast diversity of genes, their functions, and interactions. This module aims to provide students with knowledge about the structure, sequencing, and functional analysis of genomes, mainly the human genome. It includes a review of the genome structure, its mapping and sequencing, the use of model organisms, and the applications of different types of markers used to construct genetic maps, such as restriction fragment length polymorphisms (RFLPs), simple sequence length polymorphisms (SSLPs) and single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs). Methodologies such as next-generation sequencing (NGS), comparative genomics, and functional genomics will be discussed in the module. Students will also be trained in lab techniques used in detection and analysis of the genome. Towards the end of the module, students will relate the use of genomics concepts and techniques in pharmacogenomics, bioinformatics, developing genetically modified organisms, and the field of medical diagnostics. In addition, students will learn how to utilise basic bioinformatic tools to obtain and analyse DNA sequences.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
This module aims to provide students with knowledge about advances and applications in the field of genomics. It includes a review of the genome structure, its mapping and sequencing, the use of model organisms, and an understanding of the use of expression sequence tags (ESTs) and single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs), as well as techniques used to study gene functions and generate transgenic animals and mutant species. The genetic aspect of diseases, the Human Genome Project, the applications of molecular biology in the diagnosis of diseases, and the identification of genetic variations are also covered.
features: Covers biological concepts, cells, genetics, and scientific investigation through laboratory practice and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in biology, life sciences, research, and scientific problem-solving.

name: Advanced Cell Biology
description: Students will explore cellular processes in humans, with a focus on cell signalling. Interactions between biomolecules will be studied, along with drug discovery and drug targeting mechanisms. The module will provide a strong foundation in current concepts within molecular and cellular biology, especially those integral to experimental manipulation in biomedical research.
features: Covers biological concepts, cells, genetics, and scientific investigation through laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in biology, life sciences, research, and scientific problem-solving.

name: Resource Management and Circular Economy
description: This module provides students with a holistic overview of maximising the use of resources and minimising waste generation. The first part of the module exposes students to the various technologies and management techniques in the areas of solid and hazardous waste treatment, waste minimisation and resource recovery. In the second part of the module, students will be introduced to a broad range of topics on energy management such as the supply and demand of conventional energy resources, clean and renewable energy technologies, and energy auditing.
features: Covers physics principles, calculations, real-life applications, and practical activities.
suitableFor: Students interested in physics, engineering, applied science, and quantitative problem-solving.

name: Workplace Safety and Health
description:
This module aims to equip students with the fundamental knowledge and understanding of concepts to manage safety and health at workplaces. Students will be introduced to the Singapore Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) legal framework and internationally or nationally recognised safety and health management systems.

Students will learn how to identify various hazards that may be present in different workplaces, and their impact on safety and health. They will then learn how to develop risk control measures thereafter. Students will also acquire the knowledge and skills to manage hazardous substances as well as to implement and audit a WSH Management System.
features: Introduces key concepts and practical understanding in workplace safety and health through project-based work and real-world applications.
suitableFor: Students who want an introductory understanding of workplace safety and health.

name: Water Resource Management
description: Students can look forward to learn about the principles and processes used in water and wastewater treatment in this module. Topics covered include water quality and effluent discharge standard in water testing, processes and unit operations involved in producing potable water and in treating wastewater. Basic principles of membrane technology (such as properties of membrane materials, transport and polarisation phenomena), as well as its processes and applications, will also be introduced
features: Covers sports science, health, fitness, movement, and performance-related concepts through laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in sports, health promotion, exercise science, coaching, and active lifestyles.

name: Environmental Public Health
description: This module discusses the impact of the environment on the health of human populations and introduces the principles of epidemiology to students. The principles, practices and standards of sanitation in relation to the protection of public health will also be covered. Students will have the opportunity to explore the epidemiology of food-borne diseases in Singapore and address local retail food hygiene issues.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Cosmetic Product Design and Development
description: This module is designed to equip students with the necessary skills and knowledge to ideate, conceptualize and formulate a specific personal care product, such as sunscreen, moisturizer, body shampoo, or others by leveraging market research and consumer trend. Through project-based learning approach, students will revisit concepts from specialty chemicals and formulation science to develop innovative products and learn to overcome challenges encountered during product development. Students will apply practical skills and experience the iterative processes of prototyping and evaluation in the context of personal care product development and learn how to systematically refine and enhance personal care formulations, ensuring optimal product performance and consumer satisfaction.
features: Covers creative production, design thinking, media tools, and visual communication through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in design, media, storytelling, creative production, and visual expression.

name: Sustainable Production and Products
description: This module focuses on sustainable production and products with a primary emphasis on reducing carbon emissions and promoting eco-friendly technologies. It covers various topics such as carbon capture and utilization, hydrogen production and applications, renewable fuels and chemicals, and product carbon footprint analysis. Students will gain insights into the latest technologies and practices in sustainable production and the role they play in mitigating climate change.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Biological Applications in Health and Diagnostics
description: This module aims to highlight the use of biological compounds for disease diagnostics and treatment. Students will gain hands-on experience in stem cell culture and learn how cell reprogramming and bioprinting are carried out in the field of regenerative medicine. Integrating knowledge in recombinant DNA technology, students will learn to design genes to produce proteins and enzymes that can be used for disease detection and medical therapy. In addition, interactions between viruses and host will be examined for better understanding of anti-viral drug therapy, vaccine development and drug delivery. Concepts on precision medicine, an approach that customises treatment and prevention strategies, will be introduced in this module. This includes predictive diagnostic tools and ethical considerations in precision medicine research.
features: Covers biological concepts, cells, genetics, and scientific investigation through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in biology, life sciences, research, and scientific problem-solving.

name: Oncology and Therapeutics
description: This module covers the basic processes and regulation of cell proliferation, the cell cycle and cell death. An appreciation of the role of oncogenes and tumour suppressor genes is central to an understanding of the molecular basis of carcinogenesis and the properties of cancer cells. Environmental and genetic factors in cancer, tumour immunology and cancer therapies are also covered. Concepts from cell biology, genetics, immunology, histopathology, and anatomy are included. The practical component of the module, which allows students to apply the concepts covered in the theory component, will introduce students to materials, equipment and laboratory techniques that are frequently used in oncology laboratories such as flow cytometry and immunofluorescence.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility through hands-on activities and laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Nanotechnology
description: This module covers basic principles used in the study of phenomena and manipulation of materials at the atomic, molecular and macromolecular levels, where properties differ significantly from those at a larger scale. It addresses some of the simple design, characterisation, production and application of structures, devices and systems by controlling shapes and sizes at the nanometre scale. This module focuses on what causes bulk properties of materials to change dramatically with nano ingredients. It draws attention to how quantum effects can begin to dominate the behaviour of matter at the nano level, affecting the optical, electrical and magnetic behaviour of materials. Students taking this module will also learn the need for nanomaterials and the distinction between nanostructure and microstructure, as well as understand the creation of nanostructures, and the production of new materials with enhanced properties for advanced applications.
features: Covers creative production, design thinking, media tools, and visual communication through hands-on activities.
suitableFor: Students interested in design, media, storytelling, creative production, and visual expression.

name: Pollution Control and Monitoring
description:
Pollution Control and Monitoring introduces students to the conventional methods and up-to-date technologies employed in the sampling, monitoring and control of the major types of pollution. The scope of the module includes air pollution, water pollution, subsurface contamination, noise pollution, and light pollution.

Topics covered include environment-contaminant interactions, transport of pollutants, pollutant containment systems, monitoring systems, and remediation technologies. Students will also explore various techniques employed such as physical, chemical and biological sample analysis, biosensors, real-time monitoring and remote sensing. Environmental laws by regulatory bodies such as the National Environment Agency (NEA) will also be covered.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
A334 Pollution Control and Monitoring introduces students to the conventional methods and up-to-date technologies employed in the sampling, monitoring and control of the major types of pollution. The scope of the module includes air pollution, water pollution, subsurface contamination, noise pollution and light pollution.
Topics covered include environment-contaminant interactions, transport of pollutants, pollutant containment systems, monitoring systems and remediation technologies. Students will also conduct physical, chemical and biological sample analyses, real-time monitoring and remote sensing and use biosensors. Environmental laws by regulatory bodies such as the National Environment Agency (NEA) will also be covered.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility through problem-solving tasks.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Biopharmaceutical Production
description: This module focuses on the manufacturing process of biopharmaceuticals required for treatment of diseases. The development of high value protein-based drugs like antibodies, growth factors and vaccines begin with upstream processes such as cell inoculation, bioreactor operation, and harvesting of cultures. Students will later be exposed to techniques in chromatography and tangential flow filtration required for downstream processing of drugs. In this module, students will learn contamination control procedures and how to operate within a controlled clean room environment. Knowledge on Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) and Good Documentation Practice (GDP) will be applied throughout the module to reinforce the importance of product quality and safety in the biopharmaceutical industry. Concepts on using artificial intelligence for designing biologic modalities and running simulations for process optimization will be briefly covered. Key aspects related to sustainability in the industry, such as carbon emission, energy efficiency, and product life cycle management will also be explored.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Biomaterials
description: This module aims to introduce students to different classes of biomaterials used in medical applications. Students will learn about host reactions to biomaterials and biocompatibility testing of different classes of biomaterials. In particular, biomaterials used in drug delivery systems and tissue engineering scaffolds will be discussed in depth.Γö¼├í Students will also be equipped with the knowledge on how to bring a medical device from lab to market.
features: Covers laboratory practices, safety, documentation, quality standards, and compliance through laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in laboratory work, applied science operations, quality control, and safe practice.

name: Medicinal Chemistry
description:
This module builds upon foundational chemistry modules which introduce students to concepts of medicinal chemistry that are relevant for the understanding of pharmacological activities and pharmacokinetics of drug molecules. Students will gain knowledge on the chemistry of drugs and drug systems, focusing on heterocyclic chemistry and the major drug classes. Principles of drug design, with an emphasis on the relationship between structure, physicochemical properties and the molecular basis of drug action will also be taught. In addition, students will acquire practical laboratory skills in their study of the synthesis, characterisation, identification and chemical optimisation of drug molecules. Topics covered include the role of chemistry in the drug discovery process, selection and screening techniques and design of compound libraries.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
A346 Medicinal Chemistry aims to provide an overview of the concepts involved in the discovery, design, identification and preparation of biologically active compounds, the study of their metabolism, the interpretation of their mode of action at the molecular level and the construction of the relationship between chemical structure and pharmacological activity for a series of compounds. The organizational approach taken in this course is to build from the chemical, physicochemical and structural property fundamentals covered in introductory chemistry modules to reaction mechanisms before exposing students to chemical synthesis and synthetic library design. ADMET (absorption-distribution-metabolism-excretion-toxicity) and pharmacodynamic principles are also emphasized with help of case studies involving examples of therapeutic substances from synthetic and natural sources. Students will be able to apply what they have learnt in earlier modules namely A233 Pharmacology and Toxicology, A371 Pharmacotherapy and A106 Organic and Inorganic chemistry. This concept driven course will encourage learning through problem solving of case studies and understanding the application of various concepts covered.
features: Covers chemical principles, laboratory techniques, reactions, and data interpretation through hands-on activities and laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in chemistry, applied science, laboratory work, and analytical thinking.

name: Medical Microbiology
description: This module covers the principles and techniques of medical microbiology laboratory investigations, which aid clinicians in the diagnosis of diseases and the monitoring of patients╬ô├ç├û progress with treatments. Through hands on experience, students are exposed to techniques in cultivation, isolation and identification of pathogens in routine clinical laboratories. The aim of this module is to give students an overview of the investigation techniques employed by the medical microbiology laboratory, as well as the rationale behind these tests. Students also learn about good laboratory practices, as well as record keeping, literature reading, specimen analysis, results interpretation, and presentation.
features: Covers biological concepts, cells, genetics, and scientific investigation through laboratory practice and problem-solving tasks.
suitableFor: Students interested in biology, life sciences, research, and scientific problem-solving.

name: Diagnostic Pathology
description:
This module emphasises on the pathologic basis of disease development as well as the principles and techniques of histopathology and cytology laboratory investigations, which aids the clinicians in diagnosis of diseases and monitoring the progress of treatment. Through the laboratory sessions, students will be exposed to some histo-techniques involving processing of tissue specimens for diagnostic purposes in routine clinical laboratories. The aims of this module are to give the students an overview of the investigation techniques employed by the histopathology and cytology laboratory, as well as the rationale behind these tests. Students will also be familiar with Good Laboratory Practices, as well as record keeping, literature reading, results interpretation, and presentation.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
This module emphasizes on the pathologic basis of disease development as well as the principles and techniques of histopathology and cytology laboratory investigations, which aids the clinicians in diagnosis of diseases and monitoring the progress of treatment. Through the laboratory sessions, students will be exposed to some histo-techniques involving processing of tissue specimens for diagnostic purposes in routine clinical laboratories. The aims of this module are to give the students an overview of the investigation techniques employed by the histopathology and cytology laboratory, as well as the rationale behind these tests. Students will also be familiar with Good Laboratory Practices, as well as record keeping, literature reading, results interpretation, and presentation. They will also be trained in record keeping, writing laboratory reports, good laboratory practices and quality assurance.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice through laboratory practice and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Clinical Chemistry
description: This module covers the physiology of the major organ systems (e.g. renal, hepatic, gastrointestinal, cardiovascular, skeletal, muscle and connective tissue), the homeostatic control of acid-base and electrolytes, and the changes that occur as a result of diseases. Emphasis is placed on explaining the underlying biochemical disturbances or metabolic derangements in diseased states and their appropriate evaluation and diagnosis using selective techniques. Topics include diagnostic enzymology, proteins in health and disease, disorders in carbohydrates, lipid and protein metabolism (including inherited disorders), biochemical assessment in nutritional disorders, hypertension and malignancy, therapeutic drug monitoring, and toxicological screening.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Haematology
description: This module introduces the components of blood and the common disorders affecting them. It also deals with the principles and techniques of haematology laboratory investigations that are useful in the diagnosis of diseases and monitoring the progress of treatments. In the laboratory sessions, students are exposed to techniques involving the handling and processing of blood and blood products for diagnostic purposes in routine clinical laboratories. This module gives students an overview of the investigation techniques employed by the haematology laboratory and blood bank, the rationale behind these tests and the correlation with clinical conditions. Students also learn about good laboratory practices, as well as record keeping, literature reading, specimen analysis, results interpretation, and presentation.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice through laboratory practice and problem-solving tasks.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Comparative Aquatic Animal Physiology
description:
This module provides students a foundation in basic physiological processes of aquatic animals, focusing on different ways they adapt to diverse and challenging environments.

Emphasis is placed on marine animals and the integrative mechanisms which regulate their important organ systems. Students will study endocrine feedback, nitrogenous waste regulation, thermoregulation, and metabolism adaptation to salinity, oxygen, pressure and desiccation.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
A358 Comparative Aquatic Animal Physiology aims to provide students with a foundation in basic physiological processes in aquatic animals. The focus will be the different ways animals adapt to their environment. Emphasis will be placed on marine animals, and the integrative mechanisms involved in the regulation of important organ systems. Topics include endocrine feedback, nitrogenous waste regulation; thermoregulation; metabolism adaptation to salinity, oxygen, pressure and desiccation. Upon completion of this module, students should have a firm understanding of basic physiological processes in aquatic animals and an understanding of how animals adapt to the diverse and challenging environments.
features: Covers human body systems, anatomy, physiology, and biological functions.
suitableFor: Students interested in health sciences, human biology, sports science, and healthcare pathways.

name: Developmental Anatomy and Neuroscience
description:
This module provides an insight into the origin and development of embryonic and foetal life, and the effects of aging on the various organ systems. Students will study how the human nervous system and special senses like olfaction and vision work. In addition, they will learn about the musculoskeletal system with its related distribution of nerves. The module also covers in depth neurobiological functions such as speech and language comprehension, the cognitive function of memory, positive reinforcement, euphoria, sleep and related regulatory mechanisms.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
This module provides an insight into the origin and development of embryonic and fetal life, and the effects of aging on the various organ systems including neurobiological functions. It encompasses the embryonic development of nervous system in humans and physiology of special senses like olfaction, gustation, vision and more.  Students will gain deeper content knowledge on the musculoskeletal system and related nervous innervation.  The module also covers in-depth neurobiological functions such as speech and language comprehension, cognitive function of memory, positive reinforcement and euphoria and sleep and related regulatory mechanisms.
features: Covers human body systems, anatomy, physiology, and biological functions through project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in health sciences, human biology, sports science, and healthcare pathways.

name: Bioinformatics
description: This module introduces students to the fundamental concepts of bioinformatics and demonstrates the application of bioinformatics to address biological questions. The module covers how genomic, proteomic and protein structure data is stored and accessed. Students will learn about how the various bioinformatics tools and databases can be used to understand genes or proteins functions, as well as utilise these tools to understand biological processes and human diseases.
features: Covers biological concepts, cells, genetics, and scientific investigation.
suitableFor: Students interested in biology, life sciences, research, and scientific problem-solving.

name: Analytical Chemistry
description: This module introduces students to different analytical techniques used in the laboratory and to data analysis. Students will be taught various topics and will be exposed to a wide range of scientific testing methods, from extraction and simple physical and chemical tests, to the use of advanced laboratory equipment. The focus is on the basic working principles of the instruments, as well as the applications and limitations of the various techniques.
features: Covers chemical principles, laboratory techniques, reactions, and data interpretation through laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in chemistry, applied science, laboratory work, and analytical thinking.

name: Current Good Manufacturing Practice
description:
This module introduces students to the role of good manufacturing practices (GMP) in the food, pharmaceutical, biopharmaceutical and medical devices industries. They will examine approaches taken in the development of manufacturing systems, and study how quality assurance, quality control, validation and regulatory audits help in the production process. Students will also learn statistical process control and the application of Lean Six Sigma methodologies to optimize manufacturing processes, enhance quality, and ensure regulatory compliance.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
This module allows students to examine Current Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) guidelines for the regulation of food, pharmaceuticals, biopharmaceuticals and medical devices, as well as their roles in related industries. It encompasses the approaches taken in the development of manufacturing systems, quality assurance, quality control validation and regulatory audits, and covers validation plans such as cleaning validation and analytical method validation.
features: Covers engineering concepts, technical systems, applied design, and practical problem-solving through project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in engineering, technology, systems thinking, and hands-on technical work.

name: Research Apprenticeship Programme in Bio-Innovation
description: This module is designed to bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical skills, providing students with the opportunity to create and work on projects as a team. It also aims to offer students a robust blend of hands-on scientific research and cultivation of cross-disciplinary skills. Structured around an experiential learning approach, students will learn how to apply their knowledge in biological sciences to create innovative prototypes or research ideas, together with guidance and mentorship from experienced staff or industry partners. To ensure that the proposed solutions are both innovative and user-centric, students will learn how to apply design thinking methodologies to foster creativity. The curriculum also encompasses vital business knowledge, such as marketing strategies and intellectual property management, to aid in transforming lab-based discoveries into market-ready products. Through the integration of science and entrepreneurship, this apprenticeship programme aims to train students for a wider range of careers in the life science industries.
features: Covers biological concepts, cells, genetics, and scientific investigation through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in biology, life sciences, research, and scientific problem-solving.

name: Research Apprenticeship Programme in Sustainable Food Innovation
description: Sustainable food innovation is focused on developing new, environmentally friendly food products and processes. In this module, students apply knowledge from related courses to the practical aspects of product development and commercialization within the food industry. Key topics include food valorisation, upcycling, and the development of sustainable food sources like alternative proteins. Students gain insights into the properties of various food ingredients and additives, along with the challenges posed by additives. The curriculum also offers interdisciplinary training covering intellectual property rights specific to food innovations, as well as design thinking and business strategies for launching sustainable food products. Through a project-based approach, students collaborate with industry partners to develop feasible product ideas, blending scientific knowledge with practical business skills. This module aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of new product development, preparing students to innovate in the food sector with a focus on sustainability and commercial viability.
features: Covers food, nutrition, wellness, and healthy lifestyle choices through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in food, nutrition, wellness, and making healthier lifestyle decisions.

name: Aquaculture Genetics
description: This module builds on the concept of genes as the unit of heredity in organisms. Students will learn Mendelian inheritance, breeding and genetic crosses. The mechanisms behind genetic transmission as well as variation between organisms within a population will be taught. Students will then go on to learn about how genetic resources can be used to enhance aquaculture production through selective breeding, marker-assisted selection (MAS), and genetic modification (GM) to achieve desired traits in livestock including fish, shellfish, and algae. Additionally, they will be given the opportunity to gain hands-on skills in the techniques used to analyse genetic and genomic information.  Advances and new developments in the areas of Marker Assisted Selection, Genetic modification, and analytical techniques will also be covered in this module.
features: Covers biological concepts, cells, genetics, and scientific investigation through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in biology, life sciences, research, and scientific problem-solving.

name: Aquaculture Product Quality and Food Safety
description: The module will provide students with the knowledge and skills necessary for post-harvest processing of seafood to ensure food product quality and safety. The importance of postharvest technology on product quality and safety will be addressed as they gain an understanding of how food processing techniques such as smoking, freezing and canning help to extend shelf life of products. Students will learn about the principles of basic hygiene and general food safety in accordance with industry regulations. They will also gain competence in basic laboratory techniques such as enumeration of enterobacteria counts, which is an important group in the food industry for monitoring hygiene and sanitation.
features: Covers food, nutrition, wellness, and healthy lifestyle choices through laboratory practice and real-world applications.
suitableFor: Students interested in food, nutrition, wellness, and making healthier lifestyle decisions.

name: Environment, Health and Safety
description:
This module aims to equip students with the fundamental knowledge and understanding of concepts to manage environment, health, and safety issues at the workplace. Students will learn how to identify and control hazards that may be present in different workplaces, and their impact on safety and health. They will then acquire the knowledge and skills to implement and audit a Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) Management System. 
In addition, the module trains students on environmental health, covering topics from managing indoor air quality to infectious disease and vector control. This equips students with the knowledge and skills to take on the role as an environmental control coordinator. Students will also be introduced to Singapore╬ô├ç├ûs environmental and workplace safety and health legislation.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility through real-world applications.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Pharmacotherapy and Pharmacy Practice
description: Students will study the rational use of therapeutic agents in this module, and gain the necessary foundation and problem-solving skills. The module covers the use of therapeutic interventions in major medical conditions as well as contemporary practices in patient assessment and counselling, over-the-counter medication and prescription. Concepts of appropriate drug treatment and the principles of good dispensing practices are also covered.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice through problem-solving tasks.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Pharmaceutics and Compounding Techniques
description: Pharmaceutics and Compounding techniques will familiarize students with the core principles underlying drug delivery systems, formulation, and compounding of various drug dosage forms. Students will explore conventional routes of drug administration, alongside specialized delivery systems like inhalation, transdermal, ophthalmic and parenteral routes. Through hands-on practicals, students will gain competencies in preparing extemporaneous preparations, such as suspensions, emulsions, creams, lotions and suppositories .  They will also learn to apply pharmaceutical calculation skills for precise medication preparation, as well as the application of physicochemical principles to enhance drug delivery systems. Additionally, the module introduces students to aseptic compounding practices.
features: Covers chemical principles, laboratory techniques, reactions, and data interpretation through hands-on activities and problem-solving tasks.
suitableFor: Students interested in chemistry, applied science, laboratory work, and analytical thinking.

name: Drug Development and Commercialisation
description:
Through this module, students will be introduced to how the sales and marketing function can be a driving force in the pharmaceutical and medical technology market. Students will learn about the value chain of drugs and biologics, from development to commercialisation. Topics covered include pre- and post-launch processes, product life cycles, and marketing trends in the life sciences and healthcare industries. Students will also explore factors that influence sales and marketing strategies, as well as contentious issues such as regulations and the health claims of supplements and cosmeceuticals.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
A376 Drug Development and Commercialisation aims to provide an overview of pharmaceutical product value chain, which ranges from drug development, regulatory approval to pre- and post-launch of the product. The driving force behind multinational major players in the market and the factors that influence the marketing strategies applied will be explored. Students will be able to apply what they have learnt in earlier modules namely A233 Pharmacology and Toxicology, A371 Pharmacotherapy and Pharmacy Practice and A272 Fundamental and Pharmacy Practice. They will learn about the interdependence of the payers, providers and pharmaceutical value chains which influence drug launches and product life-cycle management. Students will be introduced to the drug discovery process, clinical trials and Good Clinical Practice. After learning about drug development, they will learn to apply marketing strategies and concepts, and methods employed to commercialize pharmaceutical products and pricing strategies. They will be taught to do market analysis and planning and product life-cycle management. This concept driven course will encourage learning through problem solving, and understanding the application of marketing strategies in the pharmaceutical industry. Through group discussions, interactive seminars and project work, students will demonstrate high level of creativity and professionalism when engaging contentious issues and evolving marketing trends in the pharmaceutical industry.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice through project-based work and real-world applications.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Pharmaceuticals and Biologics Manufacturing Science
description: Pharmaceuticals and Biologics Manufacturing Science introduces students to the science and technology behind the manufacturing of small molecule drugs and biologics.  Students will explore topics such as product formulation and various manufacturing technologies including granulation, spray-drying, tabletting, coating, and bioprocessing. Students will also acquire hands-on experience to manufacture tablets, perform quality control testing and analysis, gaining insights into how drugs and therapeutics are required to adhere to pharmacopoeial standards. The environmental and sustainability considerations in drug manufacturing will also be explored.
features: Covers engineering concepts, technical systems, applied design, and practical problem-solving through hands-on activities and laboratory practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in engineering, technology, systems thinking, and hands-on technical work.

name: Health Products Supply Chain
description:
The regulatory guidelines for proper distribution of dangerous goods, therapeutic products, health supplements, medicinal products and medical devices will be explored. Students will examine the various components of the supply chain, including sourcing, manufacturing, warehousing, distribution, and retail. Students will learn strategies for effective inventory management within the health products supply chain, including inventory optimization, demand forecasting, and inventory tracking systems. Special attention will be given to managing temperature-sensitive drugs and therapeutics. Students will also explore current challenges and trends impacting the health products supply chain, such as emerging technologies, sustainability, and the growing demand for personalized medicine.

[Module Synopsis for students in AY2024 intake and earlier] 
In this module, students will learn about the regulatory guidelines for proper distribution of dangerous goods, therapeutic products, health supplements, medicinal products and medical devices for human use.

Students will gain an understanding of the various roles and responsibilities in the distribution/supply chain, including support services like warehousing, manufacturing and packaging as well as the current issues and challenges faced by the healthcare supply chain industry. Students will also learn about cold chain management and how to handle specialized products like temperature-sensitive drugs and biologics.
features: Covers operations, logistics, supply chain processes, and resource coordination through real-world applications.
suitableFor: Students interested in logistics, operations, supply chain management, and process improvement.

name: Environmental Pollution Control and Technology
description: This module offers an overview of the pollution problems that occur in our environment and their impact to humans and the ecosystem.  Key concepts in pollution science such as environmental chemistry, transport pathway, dispersion modelling, preventive measures and local and international regulatory laws will be covered. Students will also learn about the technology used in wastewater treatment and contaminated soil remediation, as well as nature-based solutions. Lab sessions and field trips will be included to allow students to have a better appreciation of the concepts learnt.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility through laboratory practice and problem-solving tasks.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Advanced Pharmacotherapy
description: Advanced Pharmacotherapy builds upon foundational knowledge of pharmacology, pharmacotherapy and pharmacy practice. Students will focus on specialised pharmacological interventions for various medical conditions, including psychiatric disorders, neurological conditions, musculoskeletal ailments. Students will examine pharmacotherapeutic approaches for these disease conditions and learn the drug classes, mechanisms of action, and clinical applications of relevant drugs. Through exposure to realistic patient scenarios involving complex medical conditions and special patient populations, students will develop critical analysis skills and gain experience in evidence-based decision-making.
features: Covers human body systems, anatomy, physiology, and biological functions through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in health sciences, human biology, sports science, and healthcare pathways.

name: Clinical Development to Commercial Success
description: The module equips students with the knowledge and skills to navigate the complexities of the pharmaceutical industry effectively, from early-stage development to post-launch product management. Students will gain insights into clinical trials, regulatory pathways, marketing trends, commercialization strategies, and lifecycle management approaches. They will gain a practical understanding of the interconnected aspects of clinical development, regulatory compliance, marketing, and lifecycle management critical for achieving successful drug commercialisation and maintaining long-term market relevance.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Digital Health Innovations and Applications
description: This module aims to introduce students to the transformative impact of digital technologies on healthcare systems and practices. Students will learn key concepts and components of digital health, including electronic health records (EHRs), telehealth, and wearable devices. Student will learn about health information systems, focusing on pharmacy informatics. Students will also gain an understanding of the role of data analytics, machine learning, and artificial intelligence in healthcare for predictive modelling, decision support, and personalized medicine. These culminate into how digital technologies facilitate patient education and empowerment, promoting patient-centered care models.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Drug Design and Medicinal Chemistry
description: Drug Design and Medicinal Chemistry introduces students to the interplay between chemical structure, biological activity, and pharmacokinetic properties, laying the groundwork for understanding drug design and development processes. Students will gain insights on the computational and structure-based approaches used in the rational design of small molecule drugs and biological therapeutics, and the pivotal role these techniques play in optimizing lead compounds. Building upon foundational chemistry knowledge, this module also explores core medicinal chemistry concepts essential for comprehending the pharmacological actions and pharmacokinetic behavior of drugs.
features: Covers chemical principles, laboratory techniques, reactions, and data interpretation through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in chemistry, applied science, laboratory work, and analytical thinking.

name: Healthcare Professionalism and Patient Counselling Skills
description: This module aims to develop students in the technical and professional skills to effectively engage with patients in healthcare settings. The module focuses on cultivating professionalism, ethical conduct, and communication skills and equips students with the competencies to function in interdisciplinary healthcare teams to provide collaborative patient care. Through authentic role-plays set in hospital and retail pharmacy contexts, students will gain practical experience and hone their competencies in medication dispensing and patient counselling.
features: Covers patient care, healthcare settings, communication, and professional practice through hands-on activities and project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in healthcare, patient support, clinical environments, and people-centred service.

name: Medication Safety and Sustainability
description: Medication Safety and Sustainability explores the practices aimed at minimizing medication errors and enhancing medication safety, while also examining the ways in which sustainable healthcare practices can enhance the quality of patient care and mitigate environmental impact. Students will learn about pharmacy automation, unit-dose packaging and dispensing, barcode medication administration technology, inventory management of high-risk medications and medication reconciliation. Students will also explore environmentally conscious practices within healthcare settings, focusing on the incorporation of sustainability principles into medication prescribing, dispensing, administration, and the management of pharmaceutical waste.
features: Covers sustainability, environmental practices, and social responsibility.
suitableFor: Students interested in sustainability, environmental issues, and community impact.

name: Materials Processing and Characterisation
description: This module introduces students to the world of materials processing and characterisation, focusing on polymeric, metallic and ceramic, with relation to microstructure, properties, applications, and performance of the materials. Students are exposed to the idea of creating and how to produce the new materials with enhanced properties for advanced applications on an industrial scale. In addition, students will be trained to characterize materials' properties in accordance to ASTM standards. Students will also learn how digitalization is enabling a reinvention of manufacturing.
features: Covers engineering concepts, technical systems, applied design, and practical problem-solving through project-based work.
suitableFor: Students interested in engineering, technology, systems thinking, and hands-on technical work.

name: Materials Analysis
description: This module provides students with a foundation in the testing, characterisation and analysis of structural, electronic, photonic, and biomedical materials. With an engineering and scientific approach, the module introduces students to the various methods through which the durability and reliability of materials can be improved. It also allows students to understand the root cause of the failure problems and their prevention. Topics include materials failure investigation for various classifications of materials, the tools used in materials investigation, metallurgical failure mechanisms root causes. Students also investigate the cause of failed engineering products and propose preventive measures which require them to innovate by applying new materials with improved properties.
features: Covers hospitality, tourism, service operations, guest experience, and event contexts through laboratory practice and problem-solving tasks.

name: Composite Materials Design and Application
description: This module provides an overview of the principles of designing composite materials. With this design knowledge in mind, students will be able to appreciate the range of manufacturing processes for composite materials available and understand the interactions between manufacturing quality and performance. Students will be exposed to fabrication of composites such as wet layup, pre-preg hand lay-up and autoclave techniques. They will design composites using modelling or simulation to predict the desired outcomes in various stages of the process. Post-manufacturing processes such as finishing and painting, as well as assembly methods such as bonding and fastening will also be covered. The knowledge acquired will enable students to apply relevant advanced composite materials concepts in the development, formulation and testing of composite materials and to analyse the root cause of the failure of advanced composites components across a wide range of industries.

name: Additive Manufacturing for Applied Materials
description: This module provides an overview to Additive Manufacturing (AM), a process by which digital 3D design data is used to produce structures through layer-by-layer additive techniques, enabling new component designs and tailored structures. Students will be able to appreciate the different professional production techniques in AM which are clearly distinguished from conventional methods of material removal. Students will learn the fundamentals of AM techniques using different types of materials and select appropriate AM technologies for specific design-manufacturing applications. They will understand the operating principles, capabilities and limitations of AM methods which include fused deposition modelling, stereo lithography and laser sintering. Applications in major industries such as aerospace, automotive, medical devices, energy, electronics, and consumer products will also be explored.
